items.title: HippoCamp: Benchmarking Contextual Agents on Personal Computers
items.authors: Zhe Yang, Shulin Tian, Kairui Hu et al.
items.abstract: We present HippoCamp, a new benchmark designed to evaluate agents' capabilities on multimodal file management. Unlike existing agent benchmarks that focus on tasks like web interaction, tool use, or software automation in generic settings, HippoCamp evaluates agents in user-centric environments to m...

items.title: LAtent Phase Inference from Short time sequences using SHallow REcurrent Decoders (LAPIS-SHRED)
items.authors: Yuxuan Bao, Xingyue Zhang, J. Nathan Kutz
items.abstract: Reconstructing full spatio-temporal dynamics from sparse observations in both space and time remains a central challenge in complex systems, as measurements can be spatially incomplete and can be also limited to narrow temporal windows. Yet approximating the complete spatio-temporal trajectory is es...

items.title: Universal YOCO for Efficient Depth Scaling
items.authors: Yutao Sun, Li Dong, Tianzhu Ye et al.
items.abstract: The rise of test-time scaling has remarkably boosted the reasoning and agentic proficiency of Large Language Models (LLMs). Yet, standard Transformers struggle to scale inference-time compute efficiently, as conventional looping strategies suffer from high computational overhead and a KV cache that ...

items.title: $\texttt{YC-Bench}$: Benchmarking AI Agents for Long-Term Planning and Consistent Execution
items.authors: Muyu He, Adit Jain, Anand Kumar et al.
items.abstract: As LLM agents tackle increasingly complex tasks, a critical question is whether they can maintain strategic coherence over long horizons: planning under uncertainty, learning from delayed feedback, and adapting when early mistakes compound. We introduce $\texttt{YC-Bench}$, a benchmark that evaluate...

items.title: The Recipe Matters More Than the Kitchen:Mathematical Foundations of the AI Weather Prediction Pipeline
items.authors: Piyush Garg, Diana R. Gergel, Andrew E. Shao et al.
items.abstract: AI weather prediction has advanced rapidly, yet no unified mathematical framework explains what determines forecast skill. Existing theory addresses specific architectural choices rather than the learning pipeline as a whole, while operational evidence from 2023-2026 demonstrates that training metho...

items.title: Functional Force-Aware Retargeting from Virtual Human Demos to Soft Robot Policies
items.authors: Uksang Yoo, Mengjia Zhu, Evan Pezent et al.
items.abstract: We introduce SoftAct, a framework for teaching soft robot hands to perform human-like manipulation skills by explicitly reasoning about contact forces. Leveraging immersive virtual reality, our system captures rich human demonstrations, including hand kinematics, object motion, dense contact patches...

items.title: Collaborative Task and Path Planning for Heterogeneous Robotic Teams using Multi-Agent PPO
items.authors: Matthias Rubio, Julia Richter, Hendrik Kolvenbach et al.
items.abstract: Efficient robotic extraterrestrial exploration requires robots with diverse capabilities, ranging from scientific measurement tools to advanced locomotion. A robotic team enables the distribution of tasks over multiple specialized subsystems, each providing specific expertise to complete the mission...
